## [0.4.0] - 2026-07-02

### Added

- **User-scope Cursor rule composition**
  (`compose.cursor-rules-from-user`, opt-in). A project manifest can fold the
  user-scope (`~`) manifest's Cursor `rule` nodes into its own sync, so global
  Cursor rules take effect through the project's `.cursor/rules/` — Cursor has
  no file-addressable user-global rules home, and those nodes were previously
  surfaced only as inert-at-user-scope warnings. Composed rules are owned by
  the project ledger, so dropping the rule (or the opt-in) reclaims them via
  the normal orphan path. Id collisions resolve project-wins with a per-id
  shadow warning. Composition is best-effort: a missing or malformed user
  manifest never fails the project sync, and remote user canonicals are never
  fetched during composition. `validate`, `watch`, and `sync --workspace` see
  the same composed desired state, so composed rules are neither reported as
  drift nor deleted; when composition fires, the now-misleading "rule is inert
  at user scope" coverage warning is suppressed.

- **Cross-adapter shared-subdir co-ownership ("ADV-1")**
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Changelog

All notable changes to `agent-sync` are documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

While the project is pre-1.0, minor version bumps may include breaking changes;
the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.

## [Unreleased]

### Added

- **Cross-adapter shared-subdir co-ownership ("ADV-1")** + **pi `skill`
  support**. codex and pi both read the shared `.agents/skills/` tree; with
  per-target ledgers, a workspace targeting both previously failed because each
  adapter's drift guard saw the other's skill file as a foreign hand-edit
  (`ErrMidLifeDrift`). The engine's drift and orphan checks are now union-aware
  for shared-subdir leaves: a file claimed by any configured target's ledger is
  treated as managed, and a shared leaf a sibling still owns is neither
  swap-emptied nor orphan-deleted when a target releases it (verified across
  add / idempotent re-sync / full-remove, and a `--target`-filtered removal that
  leaves the sibling's copy intact). Owned-subdir prefixes keep the exact
  single-target behavior. The pi adapter now emits `skill` to
  `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md`, byte-identical to codex so a
  co-emitted skill matches.

- Bundled **`pi` adapter** (`@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`), the fourth bundled
  adapter. This first cut supports `agents-md`, section-merged into the
  workspace-root `AGENTS.md` (and `~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` at user scope) — it
  coexists with the codex/cursor sections in the same file. Pi's deliberate
  no-MCP stance is surfaced honestly: `mcp-server-entry` targeting pi emits a
  degradation warning citing Pi's rationale, never a dead file. `rule` and
  `plugin-reference` are unsupported (no Pi concept). `skill` is now supported
  (see the co-ownership entry above). `command` remains unsupported-but-planned:
  it needs owned-file-in-a-shared-dir swap support for Pi's flat `.pi/prompts/`
  tree — tracked as a follow-up. See `docs/adapters/pi.md`.

### Fixed

- **ADV-1 hardening: per-workspace run lock + dropped-target warning.** `sync`
  now takes one flock-backed run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`) held
  across the whole run, so concurrent syncs on a workspace serialize. This
  closes a cross-process race in shared-subdir co-ownership where two overlapping
  `--target`-filtered syncs could each defer a co-owned-leaf delete to the other
  and strand the file, and makes concurrent shared-leaf swaps orderly instead of
  one failing with `ErrStale`. `validate` (read-only) stays lock-free. Run-lock
  contention yields a clean *blocked* result (never a hard error), so a
  `--post-merge` git-hook sync still exits 0 and never breaks `git pull` (the hook
  caps its wait at a few seconds so a contended pull yields fast). Separately,
  a sync now warns when an on-disk ledger exists for a target no longer in the
  manifest (its files are stranded until `agent-sync unmanage` reclaims them) —
  the warning is non-destructive; sync never deletes a dropped target's files.

- `sync --user` now targets Claude Code's real user-config paths. The Claude
  adapter previously emitted its agents-md overlay to `~/CLAUDE.md` and its MCP
  servers to `~/.mcp.json` at user scope — neither of which Claude Code reads —
  so user-scope syncs were silently inert. The adapter is now scope-aware: at
  user scope it writes the managed section to `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and merges
  MCP entries into `~/.claude.json` (preserving all foreign keys), and suppresses
  the `.agent-sync-managed` sidecar (the MCP target is Claude's own shared file,
  not an agent-sync-owned one). Project and directory scope are unchanged.
- `sync --user` is now scope-aware for the Cursor and Codex adapters too. Codex
  agents-md targets `~/.codex/AGENTS.md` (Codex's user-global instructions path,
  not the inert `~/AGENTS.md`); its MCP (`~/.codex/config.toml`) and skills
  (`~/.agents/skills/`) were already correct. Cursor syncs user-global MCP
  servers to `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (sidecar suppressed); its `rule` and
  `agents-md` outputs are skipped at user scope because Cursor has no
  file-addressable user-global home for them (User Rules are app-settings/cloud;
  there is no global `AGENTS.md`). The skipped Cursor kinds are surfaced as
  per-scope coverage warnings rather than dead-path writes. Project and directory
  scope are unchanged.

### Changed

- Adapter protocol: the `initialize` params carry a new additive, optional
  `scope` field (`"user"` | `"project"` | `"directory"`; absent ⇒ `project`).
  Adapters use it to choose scope-appropriate output paths. Additive under the
  "freeze the frame, grow capabilities" policy — no protocol version bump; an
  adapter that ignores it behaves exactly as before.

## [0.3.0] - 2026-06-17

### Added

- Hierarchy-aware manifests. `agent-sync` discovers a `.agent-sync.yaml` at
  multiple filesystem scopes by walking up from the current directory — project
  (the nearest `.git` ancestor), any intermediate directories, and the user home
  (`~`) — and emits each manifest to its own scope. Precedence is resolved by
  each target tool's native config hierarchy (Claude Code, Codex's `AGENTS.md`
  walk, Cursor's nested `.cursor/rules`); agent-sync never merges across levels.
  New `internal/hierarchy` package.
- `sync` is multi-scope: it emits every manifest from the current directory up
  to the project root, with continue-and-report across scopes (one failing scope
  no longer blocks the others, and per-scope operational/trust failures keep
  their specific exit codes). The new `--user` flag also emits the user-level
  (`~`) manifest; a plain repo `sync` never writes under the home directory, and
  `--user` is mutually exclusive with `--workspace`.
- Coverage warnings (`internal/coverage`). `sync` warns when a scope emits a
  node kind a target tool will not read natively at that level (e.g. a
  nested-directory skill for Claude), so silently-ineffective output is surfaced
  rather than hidden. Unknown targets/kinds default to native (no false
  warnings).
- Hierarchy-aware `status`. It lists every discovered scope (user/project/
  directory) with its level, source, and per-target managed-file counts, and
  surfaces the watch-failure banner per scope.

### Notes

- `validate` and `watch` remain single-scope (nearest workspace) for now.
- The runtime filesystem-mapping fallback for tools that cannot layer a kind
  natively is deferred behind a future explicit flag; the sync engine is
  unchanged by this release.

## [0.2.0] - 2026-06-16

### Added

- In-repo local skill source (`canonical.local_dir`). A workspace can author
  skills, rules, commands, AGENTS.md, mcp, and plugins under a workspace-relative
  directory (e.g. `.agents`) and compile them straight from the working tree —
  no git, no clone, no commit pin. The source kind is a third, mutually-exclusive
  option alongside `url`/`local_path`, exempt from pinning, trust (TOFU), and
  offline-strict. Authored skills coexist with agent-sync's own emitted skills
  in the shared `.agents/skills` tree via the reserved `agent-sync-` id prefix.
  Configure with `agent-sync init --local-dir .agents`. The decoder now reads
  through an `ir.SourceTree` interface satisfied by both `git.Repository` and a
  new `internal/worktree.Reader`.

### Fixed

- `agent-sync --version` now reports the release tag injected via
  `-ldflags -X main.version` instead of fang's `unknown (built from source)`
  placeholder. `fang.Execute` overwrites `root.Version` from build info, so the
  value is now passed back through `fang.WithVersion`.

## [0.1.0] - 2026-06-16

### Added

- Apache-2.0 `LICENSE` and `NOTICE` files; license declared in `README`.
- `docs/threat-model.md` — supply-chain and filesystem-safety threat model.
- `.goreleaser.yaml` and `.github/workflows/release.yml` — reproducible
  multi-platform release packaging (darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, linux/amd64,
  linux/arm64, windows/amd64) with SHA-256 checksums and optional minisign
  signatures.
- CI coverage gate: total statement coverage is enforced against the 80% floor
  mandated by CLAUDE.md and fails the build on a drop. Added behavioral tests
  for previously-untested packages (`internal/validate`, `conformance/echo`,
  `cmd/agent-sync`) plus the adapter op round-trip, watch sync, trust store,
  and conformance assertion paths to clear it.
- Fixed a nil-pointer panic in `adapterkit.ExitError.Error()` (the `e == nil`
  guard dereferenced `e.Code` in the same branch), surfaced by the new tests.
- CI now fails loud if the real-git/real-filesystem end-to-end tests are
  skipped, via `AGENT_SYNC_REQUIRE_GIT=1`.

### Changed

- `README` tool-support table now distinguishes bundled adapters (Claude Code,
  Cursor, Codex CLI) from planned ones, instead of advertising unimplemented tiers.

### Removed

- Roo Code from the planned tool list (decommissioned upstream).

lines: the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.
-## [Unreleased]
### Added
+- **User-scope Cursor rule composition**
+  (`compose.cursor-rules-from-user`, opt-in). A project manifest can fold the
+  user-scope (`~`) manifest's Cursor `rule` nodes into its own sync, so global
+  Cursor rules take effect through the project's `.cursor/rules/` — Cursor has
+  no file-addressable user-global rules home, and those nodes were previously
+  surfaced only as inert-at-user-scope warnings. Composed rules are owned by
+  the project ledger, so dropping the rule (or the opt-in) reclaims them via
+  the normal orphan path. Id collisions resolve project-wins with a per-id
+  shadow warning. Composition is best-effort: a missing or malformed user
+  manifest never fails the project sync, and remote user canonicals are never
+  fetched during composition. `validate`, `watch`, and `sync --workspace` see
+  the same composed desired state, so composed rules are neither reported as
+  drift nor deleted; when composition fires, the now-misleading "rule is inert
+  at user scope" coverage warning is suppressed.
- **Cross-adapter shared-subdir co-ownership ("ADV-1")** + **pi `skill`
support**. codex and pi both read the shared `.agents/skills/` tree; with
per-target ledgers, a workspace targeting both previously failed because each

11	orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
12	product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
13	hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
14	enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
15	validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

18	— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
19	composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

22	`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
23	`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
24	`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →

26	rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for

29	`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
30	[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).
31	
32	**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
33	- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
34	  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
35	  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
36	- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
37	  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
38	  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
39	  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
40	  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
41	  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
42	  agents-md + skill.
43	
44	- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
45	  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
46	  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
47	  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
48	  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
49	  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
50	  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
51	  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.
52	
53	**Next candidates (in order):**
54	1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
55	   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
56	   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
57	   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
58	   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
59	   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
60	   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
61	   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
62	   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
63	   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
64	   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
65	   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
66	   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
67	   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
68	   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
69	   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
70	   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
71	   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
72	   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
73	   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
74	   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
75	   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
76	   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
77	   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
78	   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
79	   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
80	   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
81	   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
82	   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
83	   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
84	   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
85	   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
86	   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
87	   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from

91	   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project

102	**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
103	dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
104	flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
105	(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so

108	drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
109	ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
110	non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
111	on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
112	plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
113	newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
114	`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
115	chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
116	passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
117	(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
118	rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
119	Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.
120

122	shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
123	rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
124	`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
125	`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
126	incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
127	break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
128	`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
129	Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
130	the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
131	newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).
132	
133	**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
134	(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
135	lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.
136	
137	**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
138	adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.
139	
140	The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
141	+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
142	(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
143	change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

---
name: unit-16-restart-path
description: Where agent-sync resumes — MVP shipped, 3 adapters live & all scope-aware (sync --user) after #30+#31; as of 2026-06-30 next candidates are the Pi adapter or hierarchy composition; resume from latest docs/plans|handoffs/
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 9d510688-0061-4983-885f-983207f8530f
---

The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).

**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
  agents-md + skill.

- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

**Next candidates (in order):**
1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
4. Gemini adapter.

See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].

**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.

**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).

**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.

**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.

The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

---
name: unit-16-restart-path
description: Where agent-sync resumes — v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02 (pi adapter, scope-aware sync --user, ADV-1 hardening, Cursor rule composition all shipped); next candidates are pi command, --break-lock, Gemini adapter; resume from latest docs/plans|handoffs/
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 9d510688-0061-4983-885f-983207f8530f
---

The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).

**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
  agents-md + skill.

- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

**Next candidates (in order):**
1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
4. Gemini adapter.

See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].

**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.

**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).

**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.

**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.

The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

lines: enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

content.text:
**agent-sync v0.4.0 is released and verified.** https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases/tag/v0.4.0

Summary of the release:

- **Contents**: all six commits since v0.3.0 — the bundled pi adapter (#32, #33), scope-aware `sync --user` paths for Claude/Cursor/Codex (#30, #31), ADV-1 cross-adapter co-ownership plus the per-workspace run lock (#33, #34), and user-scope Cursor rule composition (#35).
- **Pre-release gate**: `go test -race ./...` all green, `golangci-lint run` clean.
- **CHANGELOG**: PR #35 had never gotten an Unreleased entry, so I wrote one (composition semantics, project-wins collisions, best-effort hermeticity, the validate/watch drift fix from the post-merge review round), then stamped the section as `[0.4.0] - 2026-07-02`. Minor bump since it's a pre-1.0 feature release.
- **Mechanics**: followed the established pattern — `docs: stamp CHANGELOG 0.4.0` commit (`7224ee9`) directly to main plus the `v0.4.0` tag. Note the push bypassed the "changes must go through a PR" branch rule (same as the 0.3.0 stamp, but GitHub flagged it, so mentioning it).
- **Publish**: the Release workflow ran goreleaser successfully in 2m12s — five platform archives plus `checksums.txt`; signing skipped as expected (no `MINISIGN_KEY` secret). I downloaded the darwin/arm64 artifact and confirmed the binary reports `agent-sync version 0.4.0`.

I also updated the restart-path memory: v0.4.0 marks hierarchy composition done, so the next candidates are pi `command` support, the `--break-lock` flag, and the Gemini adapter.

lastPrompt: Lets release the latest version of agent-sync with the latest changes

Antigravity CLI context file AGENTS.md GEMINI.md configuration

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Antigravity CLI context file AGENTS.md GEMINI.md configuration"

Links: [{"title":"Antigravity Global Rules and Gemini CLI Global Context Both Write to `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` Causing Configuration Conflicts · Issue #16058 · google-gemini/gemini-cli","url":"https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/issues/16058"},{"title":"Antigravity CLI: A Hands-On Guide to Google's Terminal Coding Agent - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/arindam_1729/antigravity-cli-a-hands-on-guide-to-googles-terminal-coding-agent-5bc7"},{"title":"context-mode/configs/antigravity/GEMINI.md at main · mksglu/context-mode","url":"https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode/blob/main/configs/antigravity/GEMINI.md"},{"title":"AGENTS.md Guide: Cross-Tool Rules for Antigravity (2026)","url":"https://agentpedia.codes/blog/antigravity-agents-md-guide"},{"title":"Migrate from Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI: Complete Guide (Deadline June 18, 2026)","url":"https://www.aimadetools.com/blog/migrate-gemini-cli-to-antigravity-cli/"},{"title":"Antigravity CLI | Arm Learning Paths","url":"https://learn.arm.com/install-guides/antigravity/"},{"title":"Build Autonomous Developer Pipelines using agents.md and skills.md in Antigravity | Google Codelabs","url":"https://codelabs.developers.google.com/autonomous-ai-developer-pipelines-antigravity"},{"title":"Antigravity Rules: Guide with AGENTS.md & Examples (2026)","url":"https://agentpedia.codes/blog/user-rules"},{"title":"How to Use Gemini 3.5 Flash with Antigravity CLI: Setup Guide","url":"https://www.aimadetools.com/blog/how-to-use-gemini-3-5-flash-with-antigravity-cli/"},{"title":"Getting Started with Google Antigravity | Google Codelabs","url":"https://codelabs.developers.google.com/getting-started-google-antigravity"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Antigravity CLI context files and configuration:

## Context Files Overview

Antigravity CLI loads configuration files including `.agents/`, `AGENTS.md`, and project-level instructions, which you can inspect with the `agy inspect` command.

## AGENTS.md vs GEMINI.md

The CLI reads `.antigravity.md` (recommended for Antigravity CLI) or `GEMINI.md` (fully supported for backward compatibility), as well as `AGENTS.md` from your active project directory. If both `.antigravity.md` and `GEMINI.md` are present, `.antigravity.md` takes precedence.

## Configuration Conflict

There's a known issue: when using both Antigravity IDE and Gemini CLI on the same system, their global configuration files conflict because both tools are hardcoded to use the exact same file path: `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`.

## Cross-Tool Setup

If your team also uses Cursor or Claude Code, put shared rules in `AGENTS.md` and Antigravity-specific overrides in `GEMINI.md`. Both `GEMINI.md` and `AGENTS.md` continue to work in Antigravity CLI with no modifications.

## Global vs Workspace Scope

Global rules live in `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` (Antigravity global rules) and `~/.gemini/AGENTS.md` (cross-tool global rules), while workspace-level files are placed in your project root.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

results:
Based on the search results, here's what I found about Antigravity CLI context files and configuration:

## Context Files Overview

Antigravity CLI loads configuration files including `.agents/`, `AGENTS.md`, and project-level instructions, which you can inspect with the `agy inspect` command.

## AGENTS.md vs GEMINI.md

The CLI reads `.antigravity.md` (recommended for Antigravity CLI) or `GEMINI.md` (fully supported for backward compatibility), as well as `AGENTS.md` from your active project directory. If both `.antigravity.md` and `GEMINI.md` are present, `.antigravity.md` takes precedence.

## Configuration Conflict

There's a known issue: when using both Antigravity IDE and Gemini CLI on the same system, their global configuration files conflict because both tools are hardcoded to use the exact same file path: `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`.

## Cross-Tool Setup

If your team also uses Cursor or Claude Code, put shared rules in `AGENTS.md` and Antigravity-specific overrides in `GEMINI.md`. Both `GEMINI.md` and `AGENTS.md` continue to work in Antigravity CLI with no modifications.

## Global vs Workspace Scope

Global rules live in `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` (Antigravity global rules) and `~/.gemini/AGENTS.md` (cross-tool global rules), while workspace-level files are placed in your project root.

Based on the search results, here's what happened with Gemini CLI and Antigravity CLI:

## Key Points

On June 18, 2026, Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist IDE extensions stopped serving requests for Google AI Pro and Ultra, as well as those using it free of charge. Google unified its efforts into Google Antigravity, which includes a powerful server-side harness and a brand-new terminal experience: Antigravity CLI.

### Who Was Affected

On June 18, 2026, Gemini CLI stopped serving requests for Google AI Pro and Ultra, as well as those using it free of charge. However, if an organization uses Gemini CLI via a Gemini Code Assist Standard or Enterprise license, or through Google Cloud, access remains unchanged.

### The Replacement

Antigravity CLI keeps the most critical features of Gemini CLI: Agent Skills, Hooks, Subagents, and Extensions (now as Antigravity plugins). However, there won't be 1:1 feature parity right out of the gate between Gemini CLI and Antigravity CLI.

### Open Source Controversy

A significant point of contention was that Antigravity CLI isn't open source - at least not from what Google has published so far, whereas Gemini CLI was an open-source project that had received thousands of community contributions.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the provided content, here's what I can confirm:

**Deprecation Timeline:** Gemini CLI stops serving requests on June 18, 2026 for free and paid consumer tiers.

**Replacement:** "Antigravity CLI" replaces Gemini CLI as part of the broader Antigravity 2.0 platform.

**Binary Name:** Not specified in the document.

**Configuration Files:** The article doesn't mention specific config filenames like AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, or .antigravity.md.

**Config Directories:** Not documented in this content.

**Feature Support:** Antigravity CLI retains "Agent Skills, Hooks, Subagents, and Extensions (now as Antigravity plugins)." MCP server support isn't mentioned.

**Continued Gemini CLI Access:** Enterprise customers with Gemini Code Assist Standard/Enterprise licenses maintain access. The article states this cohort can continue using "Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist with access to the latest Gemini models."

**Note:** The blog post focuses on consumer/enterprise transitions rather than technical implementation details. For specifics on binary names, config file formats, and directory structures, you'd need to consult the referenced technical migration documentation.

# agent-sync

[![Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync?label=release)](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases/latest)

`agent-sync` is a Go CLI that keeps AI-agent configuration for multiple tools
(Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI — and more to come) in sync from a single
Git-backed manifest. Think of it as `dotfiles` for agents: one canonical
source of skills, rules, commands, and MCP servers; native files rendered
into each tool's own conventions.

> **Status:** pre-alpha. v1 is in active development. Architecture and
> rationale live in the [requirements](docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-aienvs-agent-workspace-requirements.md)
> and [implementation plan](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md).

## Install

Download the prebuilt binary for your platform, verify its checksum, and put it
on your PATH — one command:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh | sh
```

Installs to `/usr/local/bin` (falling back to `~/.local/bin` if that isn't
writable). Pin a version or change the location with env vars:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh \
  | AGENT_SYNC_VERSION=v0.1.0 AGENT_SYNC_INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/bin" sh
```

Prefer not to pipe to a shell? Download the archive for your OS/arch from the
[Releases page](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases), verify it
against `checksums.txt`, extract the `agent-sync` binary, and move it onto your
PATH. (Windows: grab the `.zip`.) Git must be on your PATH at runtime.

## Quickstart

New to agent-sync? [`docs/quickstart.md`](docs/quickstart.md) walks through
authoring a canonical repo and running your first `init → sync → validate`,
with a copyable example under [`examples/canonical/`](examples/canonical/).

## What agent-sync does

1. **Manifests form a hierarchy.** A `.agent-sync.yaml` binds a directory to a
   canonical source and declares which target tools receive configuration. The
   source is one of: a remote Git repo (`url`) or a local clone (`local_path`),
   both pinned by commit SHA; or an in-repo working-tree directory (`local_dir`,
   e.g. `.agents`) for per-repo skills authored right in the repo — unpinned,
   and exempt from trust and offline-strict since there's nothing remote to fetch.
   `sync` walks up from the current directory (project root = nearest `.git`
   ancestor) and emits every manifest it finds, each to its own scope; the
   user-level manifest at `~` is emitted only with `sync --user`. agent-sync
   never merges across levels — each target tool resolves precedence via its own
   native config hierarchy, and `sync` warns when a scope emits a kind a tool
   won't read natively at that level.
2. **Explicit `sync`.** `agent-sync sync` materializes the pinned content,
   compiles it via per-tool adapters, stages the output, and atomically
   swaps it into each tool's reserved subdirectory (e.g.
   `.claude/rules/agent-sync/`, `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/`, `.codex/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`).
3. **Safe by default.** Pinning, offline-strict, TOFU on `(URL, SHA)`
   pairs, reserved-prefix ownership with a ledger, and atomic swap with
   rollback — designed to fail closed.
4. **Capability-honest translation.** A tool-agnostic IR (`agents-md`,
   `rule`, `skill`, `command`, `plugin-reference`, `mcp-server-entry`) is
   translated by each adapter; per-target capability reports make
   lossy translations visible.

## Supported tools

Adapters translate the tool-agnostic IR into each tool's native files. Three
adapters ship bundled today; the rest are planned, and the `Planned (tier)`
label records the support tier each will land at.

| Tool | Status |
|------|--------|
| Claude Code | ✅ Bundled |
| Cursor | ✅ Bundled |
| Codex CLI | ✅ Bundled |
| Pi (`@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`) | ✅ Bundled (agents-md; skill & command planned) |
| Gemini CLI | Planned (supported) |
| Windsurf | Planned (experimental) |
| LM Studio | Planned (experimental) |

## Requirements

- Go 1.25+
- Git (shelled out for network operations)

## Build

```bash
go build ./cmd/agent-sync
```

## Test

```bash
go test -race ./...
golangci-lint run
```

## Project layout

See [`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md)
for the complete repo layout. The short version:

```
cmd/agent-sync/    CLI entry point
internal/          All implementation packages; no external API yet
docs/              Requirements, plans, specs
```

## Contributing

See [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for guidance when
working on `agent-sync` itself (including from inside a coding agent). These
files are **in-repo agent guidance for contributors**, not output of the
sync pipeline.

## License

Licensed under the [Apache License 2.0](LICENSE). See [`NOTICE`](NOTICE) for
attribution requirements.

name: unit-16-restart-path
description: Where agent-sync resumes — v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02 (pi adapter, scope-aware sync --user, ADV-1 hardening, Cursor rule composition all shipped); next candidates are pi command, --break-lock, Gemini adapter; resume from latest docs/plans|handoffs/
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 9d510688-0061-4983-885f-983207f8530f
---

The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

**v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02** (tag `v0.4.0`, stamp commit `7224ee9`): everything
below through PR #35 is now in a published release — pi adapter, scope-aware
`sync --user` (all 4 adapters), ADV-1 co-ownership + run-lock hardening, and
user-scope Cursor rule composition. Release workflow green, binary `--version`
verified. Next open items are the "Next candidates" list below, items 2–4
(pi `command`, `--break-lock`, Gemini adapter).

**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).

**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
  agents-md + skill.

- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

**Next candidates (in order):**
1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
4. Gemini adapter.

See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].

**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.

**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).

**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.

**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.

The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

input.prompt: What context/instruction files does Antigravity CLI read from a project? Exact filenames please — is it AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, ANTIGRAVITY.md, .antigravity.md? What is the CLI binary called? What config directory does it use?

content.content:
Based on the article, here's what Antigravity CLI reads from projects:

**Context/Instruction Files:**
- `AGENTS.md` - The article states: "drop a plain-English instruction file at the root of your project. Anything you write here gets prepended to every prompt"
- `.agents/skills/` - Directory for Agent Skills
- `.agents/` - Configuration files location
- `mcp_config.json` - For Model Context Protocol server configuration

**CLI Binary Name:**
The executable is called `agy`, not `antigravity`. The article notes: "drops the binary (named `agy`, not `antigravity`) into `~/.local/bin/`"

**Config Directory:**
The configuration file location is `~/.config/antigravity/config.toml` on Unix systems. On Windows, it uses `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Antigravity\`.

The article also mentions that `agy inspect` displays "Configuration files it has loaded" and project-level instructions, making it useful for verifying which files are being read in your specific setup.

input.prompt: What project-level context/rules files does Google Antigravity (and Antigravity CLI) read? Exact filenames: AGENTS.md? GEMINI.md? ANTIGRAVITY.md? .antigravity.md? Is GEMINI.md still supported for backward compatibility? What takes precedence?

content.content:
# Google Antigravity Context Files

Based on the documentation, Antigravity reads two project-level rules files:

## Supported Filenames

1. **AGENTS.md** — The cross-tool standard (added in v1.20.3, March 2026)
2. **GEMINI.md** — Antigravity-specific overrides

Neither `ANTIGRAVITY.md` nor `.antigravity.md` are mentioned as supported formats.

## Precedence When Both Exist

The hierarchy is explicit: *"When both files exist in the same directory, Antigravity applies a clear, fixed hierarchy"* with **GEMINI.md taking the highest user priority**. The order is:

1. System Rules (immutable)
2. **GEMINI.md** (highest user priority)
3. **AGENTS.md** (cross-tool foundation)
4. `.agent/rules/` (supplemental)

When rules conflict between the two files, *"GEMINI.md always wins in Antigravity."*

## Backward Compatibility

Yes—GEMINI.md remains fully supported. The recommended setup for multi-tool teams is to maintain both: shared universal rules in AGENTS.md and Antigravity-specific behavior in GEMINI.md.

## File Locations

- **Global:** `~/.gemini/AGENTS.md` and `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`
- **Project:** `AGENTS.md` and `GEMINI.md` in the repository root
- **Nested:** Subdirectory-specific `AGENTS.md` files (when enabled)

file.content:
## Concept set

Closed set, frozen at v1 (origin requirement R11). Adding a kind is a
v2-grade change that requires a separate spec.

| Kind | What it is | Typical filename |
|---|---|---|
| `agents-md` | Repo-root project guidance file. `AGENTS.md` is the canonical name; `CLAUDE.md` / `GEMINI.md` are recognized overlays that produce additional, target-scoped `agents-md` nodes. | `AGENTS.md` |
| `rule` | A short, behavior-shaping guideline. | `rules/<id>.md` |
| `skill` | A multi-file skill bundle (markdown + optional assets). | `skills/<id>/SKILL.md` |
| `command` | A user-invokable command definition (slash-commandable). | `commands/<id>.md` |
| `plugin-reference` | A pointer to an external plugin or extension to install. | `plugins/<id>.toml` |
| `mcp-server-entry` | An MCP server registration. | `mcp/<server>.json` |

## Canonical repo layout

The decoder reads from a `SourceTree` (`internal/ir`): a narrow interface
exposing a flat tree listing (`ReadTree`) and blob contents (`BlobContent`)
at an opaque ref. Two sources satisfy it:

- **git** (`git.Repository`) — ref is the commit SHA; the canonical source is
  a normal git repository read at that commit.
- **working tree** (`internal/worktree.Reader`) — an in-repo `local_dir` (e.g.
  `.agents`) read straight from the filesystem; ref is ignored, paths are
  presented relative to the source root, and the emitted-output subtree
  (`skills/agent-sync-*`) is skipped so the source never reads its own output.

The same layout below applies regardless of source — for the working-tree
source it is rooted at `local_dir` rather than the repo root. Tree walks are
case-sensitive even on case-insensitive filesystems.

```
canonical-repo/
├── AGENTS.md                 # one agents-md node, id "agents", Targets: [] (unscoped)
├── CLAUDE.md                 # optional overlay; separate agents-md node, id "claude", Targets: [claude]
├── GEMINI.md                 # optional overlay; separate agents-md node, id "gemini", Targets: [gemini]
├── rules/
│   └── <id>.md               # one rule node per file
├── skills/
│   └── <id>/
│       ├── SKILL.md          # one skill node per directory
│       └── <asset...>        # arbitrary skill assets, traversed and hashed
├── commands/
│   └── <id>.md               # one command node per file
├── mcp/
│   └── <server>.json         # one mcp-server-entry node per file
└── plugins/
    └── <id>.toml             # one plugin-reference node per file
```

### Node IDs

`<id>` segments must match `[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-_]{0,63}`. Decoder rejects
ids that violate the pattern. ID uniqueness is enforced **per kind**
(two skills can't share an id; a skill and a rule can).

For `agents-md` overlays at the canonical repo root the id is derived
from the filename basename, lowercased, with the extension stripped:
`AGENTS.md` → `agents`, `CLAUDE.md` → `claude`, `GEMINI.md` → `gemini`.
Each file is a distinct `agents-md` node, so the per-kind uniqueness
rule is satisfied automatically. The overlay's `Targets` is set from
the filename (`[claude]` for `CLAUDE.md`, `[gemini]` for `GEMINI.md`),
unioned with any `targets:` declared in frontmatter; `AGENTS.md` itself
ships unscoped (`Targets: []`, meaning all adapters).

### Recognized files vs. unknown files

Inside each IR-owned directory the decoder enumerates files with the
expected extension and treats anything else as an error — see

input.new_string:
## [Unreleased]

### Changed

- **Gemini CLI → Antigravity CLI.** Google retired Gemini CLI on 2026-06-18
  in favor of Antigravity CLI (`agy`); only enterprise Gemini Code Assist
  licenses retain Gemini CLI access. The planned-adapter roadmap entry is now
  Antigravity CLI, and the `GEMINI.md` root overlay in canonical sources now
  scopes its `agents-md` node to the `antigravity` target instead of the
  never-shipped `gemini` one (Antigravity still reads `GEMINI.md` as its
  tool-specific overlay, so the recognized filename is unchanged; the node id
  stays `gemini`, filename-derived). No behavior change for existing
  workspaces — neither target has a bundled adapter yet, so the overlay was
  and remains inert until the Antigravity adapter lands.

## [0.4.0] - 2026-07-02

input.command:
gh pr create --title "chore: Gemini CLI → Antigravity CLI (roadmap + GEMINI.md overlay retarget)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Why

Google retired Gemini CLI on **2026-06-18** in favor of **Antigravity CLI** (`agy`); only enterprise Gemini Code Assist licenses retain Gemini CLI access ([announcement](https://developers.googleblog.com/an-important-update-transitioning-gemini-cli-to-antigravity-cli/)). agent-sync's roadmap and IR spec still referenced Gemini CLI as a planned adapter and scoped the `GEMINI.md` overlay to a `gemini` target that will now never ship.

## What

- **README**: planned-adapter table swaps Gemini CLI for Antigravity CLI; fixes the stale "Three adapters ship bundled" count (four since pi landed in #32).
- **IR decoder + spec**: the `GEMINI.md` root overlay in canonical sources now scopes its `agents-md` node to the `antigravity` target instead of `gemini`. The recognized filename is **unchanged** — Antigravity CLI still reads `GEMINI.md` as its tool-specific overlay (alongside `AGENTS.md`; `GEMINI.md` wins on conflict), verified against multiple current sources. The node id stays `gemini` (filename-derived); the spec now documents that overlay target ≠ derived id.
- **CHANGELOG**: new Unreleased entry.

Deliberately **not** added: `.antigravity.md` overlay recognition — one blog claims it exists, but the hands-on guides and Antigravity docs list only `AGENTS.md` + `GEMINI.md`, so it stays out until Google documents it.

## Behavior change

None in practice: neither `gemini` nor `antigravity` has a bundled adapter, so the overlay node was inert before and remains inert until the Antigravity adapter lands. The retarget just ensures overlays authored today apply when it does.

## Verification

- `go test -race ./...` green (TDD: retargeted the overlay test first, confirmed red, then flipped the map)
- `golangci-lint run` — 0 issues

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/REDACTED
EOF
)"
